# Standard deviation = sqrt(var(x)) — there is no stddev() in XQL

XQL has `var(x)` (variance) but NO `stddev()`/`stdev()`/`std()` — each returns a generic 500. Compute standard deviation by taking the square root of the variance in a following `alter`: `comp var(x) as v | alter sd = sqrt(v)`. Verified: for a column with values {2000..250000, avg 56500}, `var = 6.125e9` and `sqrt(var) = 78264.86`. NOTE: `var`/`stddev` are NOT available as `windowcomp` (window) functions — only `row_number`, `lag`, `avg`, `sum`, `count` window-aggregate. So a true per-row z-score (value minus global mean over global stddev) is NOT expressible purely in windowcomp; use the per-group deviation pattern instead (see [[314-per-group-deviation-anomaly]]).

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK
| comp sum(action_total_upload) as up_total, var(action_total_upload) as up_var by actor_process_image_name
| alter up_stddev = sqrt(up_var)
| sort desc up_total
```

_Stage/function syntax live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant (Advanced-auth PAPI, narrow window). The mechanics are confirmed; adapt field names to your dataset._
